description_long: This object is a vintage desktop computer setup featuring a bulky CRT monitor with a thick, rectangular frame and a slightly curved display. The monitor sits atop a matching computer tower, both in a light beige color typical of 1980s and 1990s electronics. A wired mouse and a rectangular mouse pad are positioned to the right of the computer, with visible cables connecting the peripherals to the main unit. The rear of the monitor displays horizontal ventilation slits and a protruding power or data port with a connected cable. The front panel of the computer tower includes a floppy disk drive, several buttons, indicator lights, and a set of vertical slots, likely for ventilation or expansion. The overall design is utilitarian, with hard edges and minimal decorative elements, evoking the era of early personal computing.
description: A vintage beige CRT computer monitor with a matching tower, wired mouse, and mouse pad, featuring a thick frame and utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the monitor's depth, rear ventilation slits, and a cable connected to the back, with the mouse and pad visible on the left.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the monitor's horizontal ventilation slits, rear port with cable, and the mouse pad and mouse to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the monitor's angled back, side vents, and the mouse and pad on the right.
description_view_3: Front view showing the curved CRT screen, thick bezel, front panel with buttons, indicator lights, floppy drive, and the mouse with pad below.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and made of matte beige plastic with slightly rounded corners and no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, integrated rectangular base that sits directly on the computer tower, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons and indicator lights are located on the front panel below the screen, grouped together to the right of the floppy disk drive.
attribute_port_visibility: A single port is visible at the rear of the monitor, with a cable connected.
